findings.message: Duplicate data-access layer introduced. Routes are importing from 'db.item_service' while an established data-access layer 'db.repository' already exists with the same functionality (list_items, create_item).
findings.suggestion: Remove the import of item_service and use the existing repository module instead. This eliminates duplicate data-access abstractions and maintains a single consistent data layer pattern.

findings.message: Layering violation: Route is calling fetch_all_items() from item_service instead of using the established list_items() from repository. This bypasses the established data-access layer pattern.
findings.suggestion: Replace fetch_all_items() with list_items() imported from ..db.repository to maintain consistent layering.

findings.message: Duplicate session management pattern. The create_item route manually orchestrates session lifecycle (commit + refresh) alongside add_item(), while repository.create_item() already encapsulates this entire pattern. This creates two different implementation paths for the same operation.
findings.suggestion: Use the existing repository.create_item() function which already handles session.add(), commit(), and refresh(). Remove the manual session operations from the route handler.
